meta_title: For Founders: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no coding experience (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no coding experience. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills Setup: Build SaaS Without Coding
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between vision and execution. Learn how to master Claude Code and Antigravity to build production-grade SaaS, even without coding experience.
content.answer_block: Overcoming a lack of coding experience requires leveraging agentic workflows. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you transform natural language into modular, production-ready architecture. This setup automates the boilerplate, enforces clean code patterns, and allows non-technical founders to focus entirely on business logic, validation, and rapid revenue iteration.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is paved with brilliant ideas that never saw the light of day because the founder was stuck in the 'tutorial hell' of syntax errors and dependency management. You have the vision and the market insight, but you lack the engineering leverage to turn a concept into a functional, revenue-generating asset.

Today, we break that barrier. By combining the agentic precision of Claude Code with the architectural scaffolding of Antigravity, you stop being a dreamer and start being an architect. This isn't about learning to code; it's about learning to direct an AI to build the machine that generates your wealth.

steps.title: Initialize Environment and Agent Bridge
steps.description: Establish the working directory and link your Claude CLI to your local environment to ensure the agent has full read/write access to your project files.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a clean, modular SaaS boilerplate using Next.js 14, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase. Enforce a strict separation of concerns between UI components and server-side logic.

steps.title: Define Business Logic Schema
steps.description: Translate your product requirements into a technical specification that the AI can interpret as a database schema and API structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my core business value proposition: [Insert Idea]. Create a database schema using Prisma that supports user auth, subscription tiers, and core data models.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate prisma.schema based on the business requirements provided in the prompt.'

steps.title: Iterative Component Generation
steps.description: Build the frontend layer by layer, ensuring the AI maintains design consistency across your application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build the landing page and user dashboard components. Use a clean, professional aesthetic. Ensure all components are responsive and accessible.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create landing page components based on the design system defined in the Antigravity context.'

steps.title: API and Middleware Integration
steps.description: Connect your frontend to the backend services. This step ensures your app is dynamic and handles data flow correctly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the API routes for user authentication and Stripe checkout sessions. Ensure all endpoints have proper error handling and logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement secure API routes for Stripe integration and Supabase auth middleware.'

steps.title: Testing and Production Deployment
steps.description: Execute final audits and push your code to a production environment. Use automated checks to verify functionality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run a full security audit on the codebase. Identify potential vulnerabilities and generate a deployment script for Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Perform security audit and generate deployment configuration for production.'

pro_tips: Always use the 'context-first' approach: paste your business requirements into Antigravity before asking Claude Code to write a single line of code.
Treat Claude Code as a junior developer: review every output and ask it to explain its architectural decisions to prevent 'black box' bugs.
Modularize your prompts: instead of asking for a whole app, ask for one file or service at a time to maintain high-quality token context.
Keep a 'master prompt' file in your root directory that summarizes your tech stack and coding style, allowing Claude to remain consistent across sessions.
Leverage Claude Code's memory: use the 'chat' history to iterate on previous failures rather than starting from scratch when a feature needs a tweak.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, begin the 'Feedback Loop Phase' by integrating PostHog or similar analytics to track user behavior, then use Claude Code to iterate based on real-world usage data.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer limited by the speed of your typing or the depth of your technical background. You have the tools to ship, the logic to scale, and the framework to win. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
